PowerShell(Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 9001 -State Listen -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object { Get-Process -Id $_.OwningProcess -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue } | Select-Object Id, ProcessName)
PowerShell(Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.Name -match "dcpctrl|dotnet" } | Select-Object Id, Name, StartTime | Format-Table -AutoSize)

PowerShell(Stop-Process -Id 2916 -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.Name -eq "dotnet" } | Stop-Process -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Write-Host "Processes killed")
PowerShell($procs = Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.Name -match "dcpctrl|dotnet" }; Write-Host "Count: $\($procs.Count\)"; $procs | Select-Object Id, Name, StartTime | Format-Table -AutoSize)
PowerShell($pids = \(Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.Name -match "dcpctrl|dotnet" }\).Id; Stop-Process -Id $pids -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Write-Host "Killed $\($pids.Count\) processes")
